## What type of PR is this?

- [ ] 🆕 New case
- [ ] 🐛 Fix (broken link, typo, formatting, wrong image)
- [ ] 🌐 Translation improvement
- [ ] 📝 Other (describe below)

## New Case Submission

> Skip this section if you're not adding a case.

**Category:** (one of: Portrait & Photography / Poster & Illustration / UI & Social Media Mockup / Comparison & Community)

**Title:** (3–8 words describing the subject/style — not a truncated prompt, no leading verb like "Create…")

**Author & source link:** (the original creator + a link to the post/article, e.g. `https://x.com/...`)

**Prompt** (optional — include **only if the original author published the exact prompt**; never guess or reconstruct one):

```
Paste the exact published prompt here, in its original language. Leave this section out entirely if no prompt was published.
```

**Images:**

- [ ] Committed under `images/<category>_case<n>/` (e.g. `images/portrait_case3/output.jpg`), not hotlinked
- [ ] Multiple images named `output.jpg`, `output2.jpg`, `output3.jpg`, … (a 2×2 grid uses four)

## Checklist

- [ ] Case heading follows the format: `### Case N: [Title](source-link) (by [@author](author-link))` — attribution lives in the title (no separate `Source:` line, no type badge)
- [ ] If a prompt is included, it's inside a fenced code block (` ``` `) and is the author's actual published prompt
- [ ] Image files are committed in the repo (not external URLs); multiple images render as a `<table>` 2×2 gallery
- [ ] Added to the matching category section, and the `📑 Menu` entry + case count badge are updated
- [ ] No duplicate of an existing case (checked the source link against current cases)
